{"step_by_step_thinking": "Thalidomide is classified as an immunomodulatory drug based on multiple sources. Document [1] explicitly states that thalidomide is in the immunomodulatory class of medications and has anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and anti-angiogenic activities. Document [19] confirms that thalidomide is an immunomodulatory agent that inhibits tumor necrosis factor-alpha. Document [13] mentions thalidomide as a novel immunomodulatory agent in the treatment of Castleman disease. Additionally, Document [2] notes that thalidomide's repurposing in the 1990s was due to its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. These references confirm that thalidomide is currently used as an immunomodulatory drug in the treatment of conditions such as multiple myeloma, erythema nodosum leprosum, graft-versus-host disease, and lupus pernio. Although it has significant teratogenic risks and side effects, its use as an immunomodulatory agent is well-established in modern medicine.
